headline: Clearview AI confirmation cycle. Vermont AG Charity Clark refiled lawsuit April 25 2026 — within window — alleging Clearview maps Vermonters' faces (including children) without knowledge and sells data; suit demands permanent injunction, deletion, restitution, disgorgement, and $10K-per-violation civil penalties. Distinct from prior dismissal which was on venue grounds. Re-filed in Washington County. National class-action settlement collapse risk continues per Seventh Circuit oral arguments. European criminal complaint by noyb continues. May 2 cycle assessed Clearview at 11.6 (sub-threshold +0.7); current cycle confirms that posture without further movement.
key_evidence: April 25 2026: Vermont AG Clark refiled lawsuit in Washington County alleging Clearview maps Vermonters' faces (including children) without knowledge and sells data.
Lawsuit demands: permanent injunction, deletion of all existing data, restitution, disgorgement of profits, $10K-per-violation civil penalties.
Prior Vermont action dismissed on venue grounds in December 2025 — refiling addresses jurisdictional defect.
Seventh Circuit oral arguments raised process concerns about national class-action equity-based settlement structure.
European criminal complaint by noyb continues; multi-jurisdiction GDPR enforcement actions ongoing (Italy, France, UK, Greece, Hungary).
Business model continues: scraping public web images for biometric template database without consent; no opt-out mechanism.

evidence.finding: Vermont AG refiled lawsuit alleging Clearview maps Vermonters' faces including children without knowledge and sells data to customers.
Refiling explicitly addresses prior venue-based dismissal; Vermont seeks substantive review of biometric privacy violations.
Seventh Circuit oral arguments raised potential settlement-collapse risk on process grounds.

scoring_rationale.EQU: 1.5 unchanged. Vermont children, immigrants, protesters disproportionately affected.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1.7 (+0.2 from May 2 baseline). AB1 Harm Acknowledgment partially elevated by court-supervised equity-based settlement structure (March 2025); offset by Vermont AG refiling and ongoing EU GDPR enforcement actions.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1.5 unchanged. I1 Consistency: continues scraping despite multiple jurisdictional bans.

math_check.validator_check: +0.7 vs published, sub-threshold; Critical band sustained. May 2 ACC adjustment confirmed.

negativeSignals: Vermont AG refiled child-harm lawsuit; ongoing EU GDPR enforcement; noyb criminal complaint pending; consent-free scraping business model continues; settlement collapse risk.
positiveSignals: Court-approved equity-based class settlement (innovative harm-acknowledgment structure, contingent value).
sector_context: Clearview AI sits in highest-jurisdiction-count enforcement cluster alongside xAI/Grok. Vermont's refiling demonstrates state-level enforcement persistence even after national class-action settlement.
open_actions_resolved: Vermont refiled lawsuit captured as in-window negative evidence
Settlement-collapse risk captured as ACC notation
open_actions_carried: DHS contract child-protection use validation: not yet provided
